text: Hope as Restorative for Human Suffering? or as a Refuge from it? This consultation will try to establish how the resource of hope might be understood in relation to its potential for transforming suffering on the one hand and evacuating us from its presence on the other. Of particular concern will be the form of Christian hope understood in relation to the resurrection of the Lord, which we describe as the theologia resurrectionis—glory without becoming cheap triumphalism, glory that is more than the theology of glory! By bringing all things under the resurrection we have a whole new ontology for hope as a kind of dynamic re-presentation of the human person in the world for care. Bringing hope beyond the borders of ethnicity, sex, social status, the resurrection affirms human identity as essentially eschatological—the humanity as * creatura* within the promise of the new creation. Hope care as care for human being thereby fosters new forms of being in the world that empowers us with a fortitude for living life with hardship in between.
